evals.name: direct-audit-request
evals.prompt: Do a deep audit of a large sample of my recent Claude Code sessions. Analyze how I actually use Claude Code and find the issues and opportunities that would meaningfully improve my workflow. I don't want marginal observations — I want the few changes that would lead to step-change improvements in how I work with Claude Code. Scope it so it finishes in under 20 minutes, but do the best analysis you can within that time.
evals.expected_output: Short prioritized report with 2-4 step-change recommendations, each with quantitative evidence, specific session-id citations, and drafted implementations ready to install.

assertions.text: An outputs/report.md file exists and is at least 800 bytes
The run sampled or processed at least 10 distinct sessions (inventory/sample JSONs show >=10 sessions OR report references >=10 sessions)
Report cites at least 2 distinct 8-char+ session-id hex/UUID fragments as evidence
Report contains at least 3 numeric counts (patterns like 'N of M', 'N sessions', 'N corrections', percentages)
outputs/drafts/ (or outputs/audit/drafts/) directory exists with at least one file (SKILL.md, hook, CLAUDE.md additions, or settings diff)
Report presents a bounded top-list of 2-5 recommendations (not 20+ observations)
Recommendations are leverage-style (correction→rule, workflow→skill, tool→hook) rather than 'try harder' advice
Drafted implementations are concrete and ready to paste in (not pseudocode / placeholder text)
Evidence quotes/session-ids point to real sessions, not fabricated

evals.name: casual-phrasing
evals.prompt: I've been using Claude Code a ton lately across a bunch of projects. Can you look back at my recent usage and figure out what I should change? I want to get more leverage out of this tool — focus on big wins, not polish. Cap your work at ~20 minutes.
evals.expected_output: Same shape as eval 1 — casual phrasing should still trigger a methodical audit with drafted implementations.

assertions.text: An outputs/report.md file exists and is at least 800 bytes
The run sampled or processed at least 10 distinct sessions
Report cites at least 2 distinct 8-char+ session-id fragments as evidence
Report contains at least 3 numeric counts
drafts/ directory exists with at least one file
Approach is systematic (inventory, sample, extract, synthesize) not ad-hoc
Report presents a bounded top-list of 2-5 recommendations
Recommendations are leverage-style rather than 'try harder' advice
Drafted implementations are concrete and ready to paste in

evals.name: specific-leverage-framing
evals.prompt: I want to find step-change improvements in my Claude Code workflow. Analyze my recent sessions forensically — look at patterns in what I correct, what I repeat, what I miss — and tell me the 3 highest-leverage changes I could make to my setup. Draft the actual implementations (skills / CLAUDE.md rules / hooks) so I can install them directly. Budget: 20 minutes.
evals.expected_output: Exactly 3 high-leverage recommendations with drafted implementations ready to install.

assertions.text: An outputs/report.md file exists and is at least 800 bytes
The run sampled or processed at least 10 distinct sessions
Report cites at least 2 distinct 8-char+ session-id fragments as evidence
Report contains at least 3 numeric counts
drafts/ directory exists with at least one file
Report has exactly 3 top recommendations (user explicitly requested 3)
Each of the 3 recommendations has a corresponding drafted implementation
Recommendations are leverage-style rather than 'try harder' advice
Drafted implementations are concrete and ready to paste in
